name: H3K4me3 complex deficiency extended C. elegans lifespan
summary: H3K4 trimethylation complex genetics support chromatin-state control of lifespan in worms.
statement: Deficiencies in the H3K4 trimethylation complex members ASH-2, WDR-5, and SET-2 extended C. elegans lifespan, while the H3K4 demethylase RBR-2 was required for normal lifespan.
population_or_model: Caenorhabditis elegans H3K4me3 RNAi and mutant lifespan assays
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports lifespan extension but does not provide one percentage effect size.
caveats: This is worm genetic evidence, not a drug intervention.
The mechanism required an intact adult germline and mature egg production, limiting broad tissue translation.
H3K4me3 state is developmentally important and could carry safety tradeoffs.
